devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । इति ब्रुवाणं नृपतिं गायका मुनिचोदिताः । तुष्टुवुस्तुष्टमनसः तद् वाग् अमृतसेवया ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca iti bruvāṇaṃ nṛpatiṃ gāyakā municoditāḥ tuṣṭuvustuṣṭamanasaḥ tad vāg amṛtasevayā

translation:
Maitreya said:
The bards were satisfied at heart by hearing his nectarlike words. Being urged by sages, they began to extol the king who was speaking thus.

devanagari: नालं वयं ते महिमानुवर्णने । यो देववर्योऽवततार मायया । वेनाङ्गजातस्य च पौरुषाणि ते । वाचस्पतीनामपि बभ्रमुर्धियः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: nālaṃ vayaṃ te mahimānuvarṇane yo devavaryo venāṅgajātasya ca pauruṣāṇi te vācaspatīnāmapi babhramurdhiyaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first dual], [perfect active second plural]
[perfect active first dual], [perfect active second plural]

[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: We are competent to describe fully your glory as you are the Supreme God who have incarnated in this world by your Māyā (power). The intelligence of even masters of speech (like god Brahmā and others) has been perplexed in describing the exploits of you who are born from the arms of Vena.

devanagari: अथाप्युदारश्रवसः पृथोर्हरेः । कलावतारस्य कथामृतादृताः । यथोपदेशं मुनिभिः प्रचोदिताः । श्लाघ्यानि कर्माणि वयं वितन्महि ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: athāpyudāraśravasaḥ pṛthorhareḥ kalāvatārasya kathāmṛtādṛtāḥ yathopadeśaṃ munibhiḥ pracoditāḥ ślāghyāni karmāṇi vayaṃ vitanmahi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ ślāgh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ ślāgh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ ślāgh class 1 verb]

translation: We are, however, devoutly interested in the nectarlike stories of Pṛthu, of glorious renown, who is an incarnation of an aṃśa (part) of Hari. We are instructed by sages. We broadcast far and wide the praiseworthy deeds, according to the instructions of the sages.

devanagari: एष धर्मभृतां श्रेष्ठो लोकं धर्मेऽनुवर्तयन् । गोप्ता च धर्मसेतूनां शास्ता तत्परिपन्थिनाम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa dharmabhṛtāṃ śreṣṭho lokaṃ dharme goptā ca dharmasetūnāṃ śāstā tatparipanthinām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: This (king Pṛthu) is the foremost among the upholders of religion. He makes the people act according to Dharma (righteous path). He is the protector of the established institutions of religion and morality. He is the punisher of those who are antagonistic (to religion).

devanagari: एष वै लोकपालानां बिभर्त्येकस्तनौ तनूः । काले काले यथाभागं लोकयोः उभयोर्हितम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa vai lokapālānāṃ bibhartyekastanau tanūḥ kāle kāle yathābhāgaṃ lokayoḥ ubhayorhitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ hi class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ hi class 5 verb]

translation: (Though) he is one, he bears in his person the bodies (i.e. the powers and functions) of the guardians of the world from time to time, as per needs (of the situation) for the public good, both here and hereafter.

devanagari: वसु काल उपादत्ते काले चायं विमुञ्चति । समः सर्वेषु भूतेषु प्रतपन् सूर्यवद्विभुः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: vasu kāla upādatte kāle cāyaṃ vimuñcati samaḥ sarveṣu bhūteṣu pratapan sūryavadvibhuḥ
translation: [1] He is impartial to all beings. This mighty monarch collects taxes and tributes at proper times, and releases (the fund) at the opportune time (like famine etc.), just as the bright Sun (evaporates the waters in the summer and returns it as showers of water in the rainy season).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
From this verse onwards the king’s execution of the functions of the eight protectors of the world (lokapālas), is described.

devanagari: तितिक्षत्यक्रमं वैन्य उपर्याक्रमतामपि । भूतानां करुणः शश्वद् आर्तानां क्षितिवृत्तिमान् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: titikṣatyakramaṃ vainya uparyākramatāmapi bhūtānāṃ karuṇaḥ śaśvad ārtānāṃ kṣitivṛttimān
translation: The behaviour of the king is like the goddess of the earth. The compassionate son of Vena (i.e. Pṛthu) always bears the faults of the distressed beings, though they may encroach upon him.

devanagari: देवेऽवर्षत्यसौ देवो नरदेववपुर्हरिः । कृच्छ्रप्राणाः प्रजा ह्येष रक्षिष्यति अञ्जसेन्द्रवत् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: deve kṛcchraprāṇāḥ prajā hyeṣa rakṣiṣyati añjasendravat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: He is veritable Hari who has assumed the body of the king. When the rain-god withholds showers of rain, he would like Indra, easily protect the subjects whose life was in danger.

devanagari: आप्याययत्यसौ लोकं वदनामृतमूर्तिना । सानुरागावलोकेन विशदस्मितचारुणा ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: āpyāyayatyasau lokaṃ vadanāmṛtamūrtinā sānurāgāvalokena viśadasmitacāruṇā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [locative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: He pleases the world with his moon-like face—the embodiment of nectar—beaming with charming smiles and brightened with affectionate looks.

devanagari: अव्यक्तवर्त्मैष निगूढकार्यो । गम्भीरवेधा उपगुप्तवित्तः । अनन्तमाहात्म्यगुणैकधामा । पृथुः प्रचेता इव संवृतात्मा ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: avyaktavartmaiṣa nigūḍhakāryo gambhīravedhā upaguptavittaḥ anantamāhātmyaguṇaikadhāmā pṛthuḥ pracetā iva saṃvṛtātmā
translation: His ways are inscrutable; his acts are secret and unsurmisable; his intentions are too deep to be comprehended; he strictly guards his wealth; he is the sole, receptacle of infinite greatness and innumerable excellent attributes; like Varuṇa, the Lord of Waters, Pṛthu’s true nature is unfathomable (hidden from the public view).

devanagari: दुरासदो दुर्विषह आसन्नोऽपि विदूरवत् । नैवाभिभवितुं शक्यो वेनारण्युत्थितोऽनलः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: durāsado durviṣaha āsanno naivābhibhavituṃ śakyo venāraṇyutthito

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ru class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ ru class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ru class 1 verb]

translation: He is (like) the fire born from (the friction of) the araṇi (piece of Śamī wood) in the form of Vena (for he was born after churning Vena’s arm). He is (even mentally) unapproachable to his enemies. His overwhelming power is irresistible by his enemies. Though he be near, he is as if at a great distance (as he is well-guarded by his security force) It is impossible to overpower him.

devanagari: अन्तर्बहिश्च भूतानां पश्यन् कर्माणि चारणैः । उदासीन इवाध्यक्षो वायुरात्मेव देहिनाम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: antarbahiśca bhūtānāṃ paśyan karmāṇi cāraṇaiḥ udāsīna ivādhyakṣo vāyurātmeva dehinām
translation: Though he sees the overt and covert activities of human beings through his intelligence service (spies), he behaves as if he is indifferent (to praise or blame). He is thus like the vital air, the Soul that witnesses, as if unconcerned, the inner and outer activities of all.

devanagari: नादण्ड्यं दण्डयत्येष सुतमात्मद्विषामपि । दण्डयत्यात्मजमपि दण्ड्यं धर्मपथे स्थितः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: nādaṇḍyaṃ daṇḍayatyeṣa sutamātmadviṣāmapi daṇḍayatyātmajamapi daṇḍyaṃ dharmapathe sthitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [locative single from √ i class 2 verb]

[nominative dual from √ yat class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ yat class 10 verb], [vocative dual from √ yat class 10 verb], [accusative dual from √ yat class 10 verb]
[imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ su class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative single from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: He is so firmly established in the path of dharma (royal duties) that he never punishes anyone if he does not deserve punishment even though he be the son of his personal enemy; and that he would punish his own son, if found guilty (and worth punishing).

devanagari: अस्याप्रतिहतं चक्रं पृथोरामानसाचलात् । वर्तते भगवानर्को यावत्तपति गोगणैः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: asyāpratihataṃ cakraṃ pṛthorāmānasācalāt vartate bhagavānarko yāvattapati gogaṇaiḥ
translation: His unchallenged cakra (sway, march of his army or the path of his chariot) extends from the Mānasottara mountain[2] to all regions where the Sun-god shines by his rays.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Mānasottara.—A hill of Puṣkaraḍvīpa to the east of Meru. Devadhānī, the city of Indra and other cities are there; like the wheel of an oilmill, the wheel of Sūrya’s chariot is said to rotate on the hill as its axis (Purāṇa Index. 2. 686), A.B.L. Awasthi’s identification of this mount with the northern portion of Mānasādri, the neighbouring hill overlooking Mānasa lake (Studies in Skanda Purāṇa, p. 138) is purely conjectural.

devanagari: रञ्जयिष्यति यल्लोकं अयं आत्मविचेष्टितैः । अथ अमुं आहू राजानं मनोरञ्जनकैः प्रजाः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: rañjayiṣyati yallokaṃ ayaṃ ātmaviceṣṭitaiḥ atha amuṃ āhū rājānaṃ manorañjanakaiḥ prajāḥ
translation: He will delight and bring happiness to all people by his acts. It is on account of his acts of promoting happiness (and welfare of the people) that his subjects will call him rājan (lit. one who pleases, i.e. the king).[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Cf. yathā prahlādanāccandraḥ pratāpāt tapano yathā /
tathaiva so'bhud anvartho rājā prakṛti-rañjanāt // Raghuvaṃśa. 4.12

devanagari: दृढव्रतः सत्यसन्धो ब्रह्मण्यो वृद्धसेवकः । शरण्यः सर्वभूतानां मानदो दीनवत्सलः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛḍhavrataḥ satyasandho brahmaṇyo vṛddhasevakaḥ śaraṇyaḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ mānado dīnavatsalaḥ
translation: He is firm in his vows; true to his words; a friend of Brāhmaṇas (who are devoted to the studies of Vedas); a servant of aged people; the shelter of all beings; paying respect (where due) and kind to the afflicted.

devanagari: मातृभक्तिः परस्त्रीषु पत्‍न्यामर्ध इवात्मनः । प्रजासु पितृवत् स्निग्धः किङ्करो ब्रह्मवादिनाम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: mātṛbhaktiḥ parastrīṣu pat‍nyāmardha ivātmanaḥ prajāsu pitṛvat snigdhaḥ kiṅkaro brahmavādinām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ snih class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ snih class 4 verb]

translation: He shows the same regard and respect to other women as he would to his mother. He treats his wife as if she were half of his own person. He is affectionate to his subjects like a father. He behaves (respectfully) like a servant, to those who are well-versed in the Vedas.

devanagari: देहिनां आत्मवत्प्रेष्ठः सुहृदां नन्दिवर्धनः । मुक्तसङ्गप्रसङ्गोऽयं दण्डपाणिः असाधुषु ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: dehināṃ ātmavatpreṣṭhaḥ suhṛdāṃ nandivardhanaḥ muktasaṅgaprasaṅgo
translation:
He is most beloved to all creatures as if he was their own Self. He enhances the delight of his friends and well-wishers. He is especially attached to the company of those who are free from worldly attachments. He wields his rod of punishment against the evil-minded (miscreants).
18A.[4] By dint of his great personal power, he will make the good earth produce all the plants (as one milks the cow). He will make this earth flat by the end of his bow.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
This verse is included here, being authenticated by Bhāgavata Candrikā, Padaratnāvalī, Siddhāntapradīpa and Bhaktamanorañjanī who have commented on it. It is found in the Southern recension of the Bhāgavata Purāṇa But Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, Dīpinī, Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa Anvitārthaprakāśikā. and Bālaprabodhini have ignored it in their comments.

devanagari: अयं तु साक्षाद्‍भगवान् त्र्यधीशः । कूटस्थ आत्मा कलयावतीर्णः । यस्मिन् अविद्यारचितं निरर्थकं । पश्यन्ति नानात्वमपि प्रतीतम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: ayaṃ tu sākṣād‍bhagavān tryadhīśaḥ kūṭastha ātmā kalayāvatīrṇaḥ yasmin avidyāracitaṃ nirarthakaṃ paśyanti nānātvamapi pratītam
translation: But really he is directly the Supreme Lord himself who has incarnated by his kalā (portion, part)—the Supreme Lord who governs the three worlds; is the unchangeable perpetual inner controller of all beings; even the apparent multiplicity created in whom by nescience is rendered substanceless (and unmeaningful) when he is realized.

devanagari: अयं भुवो मण्डलमोदयाद्रेः । गोप्तैकवीरो नरदेवनाथः । आस्थाय जैत्रं रथमात्तचापः । पर्यस्यते दक्षिणतो यथार्कः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: ayaṃ bhuvo maṇḍalamodayādreḥ goptaikavīro naradevanāthaḥ āsthāya jaitraṃ rathamāttacāpaḥ paryasyate dakṣiṇato yathārkaḥ
translation: This matchless warrior, the King of kings, is the sole protector of the sphere of the earth, from the Udaya mountain with a bow in hand, and seated in his triumphant chariot, he will go round the earth from right to left, just as the Sun does.

devanagari: अस्मै नृपालाः किल तत्र तत्र । बलिं हरिष्यन्ति सलोकपालाः । मंस्यन्त एषां स्त्रिय आदिराजं । चक्रायुधं तद्यश उद्धरन्त्यः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: asmai nṛpālāḥ kila tatra tatra baliṃ hariṣyanti salokapālāḥ maṃsyanta eṣāṃ striya ādirājaṃ cakrāyudhaṃ tadyaśa uddharantyaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ hṝ class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṝ class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ hṝ class 9 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sā class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ sā class 4 verb]

translation: Kings, as well as the guardians to the quarters of the world, will certainly pay tribute to him at every place. Their queens will regard this first king as Lord Viṣṇu (the god whose weapon is cakra—discus), and sing of his glory.

devanagari: अयं महीं गां दुदुहेऽधिराजः । प्रजापतिर्वृत्तिकरः प्रजानाम् । यो लीलयाद्रीन् स्वशरासकोट्या । भिन्दन् समां गामकरोद्यथेन्द्रः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: ayaṃ mahīṃ gāṃ duduhe prajāpatirvṛttikaraḥ prajānām yo līlayādrīn svaśarāsakoṭyā bhindan samāṃ gāmakarodyathendraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[nominative single from √ kuṭ class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ kuṭ class 6 verb]
[imperative active first plural], [injunctive active first plural]

translation: This King of kings, the protector of subjects with a view to providing means of livelihood to his subjects, will milk the earth in the form of a cow. Like Indra, he will break down the mountains with the point of his bow, and level the earth as if in a sport.

devanagari: विस्फूर्जयन्नाजगवं धनुः स्वयं । यदाचरत्क्ष्मामविषह्यमाजौ । तदा निलिल्युर्दिशि दिश्यसन्तो । लाङ्गूलमुद्यम्य यथा मृगेन्द्रः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: visphūrjayannājagavaṃ dhanuḥ svayaṃ yadācaratkṣmāmaviṣahyamājau tadā nililyurdiśi diśyasanto lāṅgūlamudyamya yathā mṛgendraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Twanging his irresistible ājagava bow in the battle, when he, like a lion with his tail erected, traverses the earth, the evil-doers will hide themselves in all directions.

devanagari: एषोऽश्वमेधान् शतमाजहार । सरस्वती प्रादुरभावि यत्र । अहारषीद्यस्य हयं पुरन्दरः । शतक्रतुश्चरमे वर्तमाने ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣo sarasvatī prādurabhāvi yatra ahāraṣīdyasya hayaṃ purandaraḥ śatakratuścarame vartamāne

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[nominative dual from √ vṛt class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vṛt class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vṛt class 1 verb], [locative single from √ vṛt class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ vṛt class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vṛt class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vṛt class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vṛt class 1 verb]

translation: He will perform a hundred horse-sacrifices (aśvamedha) at the source of the river Sarasvatī. While the last sacrifice will be performed, Indra (the performer of hundred aśvamedha sacrifices) will steal away his sacrificial horse.

devanagari: एष स्वसद्मोपवने समेत्य । सनत्कुमारं भगवन्तमेकम् । आराध्य भक्त्यालभतामलं तद् । ज्ञानं यतो ब्रह्म परं विदन्ति ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa svasadmopavane sametya sanatkumāraṃ bhagavantamekam ārādhya bhaktyālabhatāmalaṃ tad jñānaṃ yato brahma paraṃ vidanti

[present middle first single], [imperative active second single]
[accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vid class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ vid class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ vid class 2 verb]

translation: He will meet the venerable Sanatkumāra alone in the garden of his palace. He will propitiate him (Sanatkumāra) with devotion. He will get from him the pure knowledge which leads one to attain to the Supreme Brahman.

devanagari: तत्र तत्र गिरस्तास्ता इति विश्रुतविक्रमः । श्रोष्यत्यात्माश्रिता गाथाः पृथुः पृथुपराक्रमः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra tatra girastāstā iti viśrutavikramaḥ śroṣyatyātmāśritā gāthāḥ pṛthuḥ pṛthuparākramaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ śru class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ śru class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ śru class 5 verb], [locative single from √ śru class 5 verb]

translation: Wherever he will go, Pṛthu, of celebrated glory and enormous prowess, will hear various songs and ballads and praise based on his[5] exploits.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
ātmāśrīta: pertaining to Viṣṇu (Bhāvāratha Dīpikā).

devanagari: दिशो विजित्याप्रतिरुद्धचक्रः । स्वतेजसोत्पाटितलोकशल्यः । सुरासुरेन्द्रैरुपगीयमान । महानुभावो भविता पतिर्भुवः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: diśo vijityāpratiruddhacakraḥ svatejasotpāṭitalokaśalyaḥ surāsurendrairupagīyamāna mahānubhāvo bhavitā patirbhuvaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ man class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[vocative single from √ man class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation:
His cakra (sway, army, wheels of his chariot) will be unobstructed and unchallenged. After conquering all the quarters of the earth, he will remove the evil-doers of the world by his heroic lustre. He will be the supreme master of the world whose great glories will be sung by the chiefs of gods and demons.”
